codeReview.summary: Thorough audit of 3 kernel files. Two genuine bugs found and correctly fixed: (1) timer deadline overflow via saturating_add, (2) missing DSB+ISB barriers in GIC init functions. SAFETY comments added to all 11 unsafe blocks (6 in interrupt_controller.rs, 5 in timer.rs). AUDIT comments added to all 3 files. 26 well-structured tests covering deadline overflow, counter_to_ns, GIC register offsets, interrupt table logic, and barrier patterns. All fixes are correct and align with ARM architecture requirements.

issues.description: Commit message says 'Added SAFETY comments to all 9 unsafe blocks (4+2 in interrupt_controller.rs, 5 in timer.rs)' but 4+2+5=11, not 9. The handoff correctly reports 11. Minor commit message typo.

summary: PASS. Both bug fixes are genuine improvements: saturating_add prevents timer deadline overflow near u64::MAX, and DSB+ISB barriers in init_distributor/init_cpu_interface prevent configuration races per ARM GIC spec. All 11 unsafe blocks have accurate SAFETY comments explaining invariants (nomem omission rationale, nostack correctness, hardware side effects). interrupt.rs correctly identified as pure safe Rust with sound two-phase wake pattern. 26 tests follow TDD discipline with buggy/fixed pairs demonstrating each finding. Test architecture correctly duplicates pure logic for host-side testing. Feature description items all addressed: GIC register access patterns verified, DSB/ISB barriers confirmed, timer overflow fixed, timer callback delivery (two-phase wake) confirmed correct, timer cancellation races (IrqMutex prevents) confirmed sound, interrupt table logic tested. One non-blocking commit message typo (says 9 unsafe blocks, should be 11).
